content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer nnn and a string sss consisting only of characters 'a' and 'b'. Consider a string t=s+s+‚ãØ+s‚èün timest = \underbrace{s + s + \dots + s}_{n\text{ times}}t=n timess+s+‚ãØ+s‚Äã‚Äã, i.e. the string obtained by concatenating nnn copies of sss.

Find the number of non-empty prefixes of ttt in which the number of occurrences of 'a' is strictly greater than the number of occurrences of 'b'.
constraints: each character of sss is either 'a' or 'b'
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains the string sss and the integer nnn, separated by a single space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of valid prefixes.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The string ttt is "abaaba". It has five prefixes which contain more a-s than b-s: "a", "aba", "abaa", "abaab" and "abaaba".
Example case 2: The string ttt is "baabaabaa". The strings "baa", "baaba", "baabaa", "baabaab", "baabaaba" and "baabaabaa" are the six valid prefixes.
Example case 3: The string ttt is "bbabbabba". There is no prefix of this string which consists of more a-s than b-s. Therefore, the answer is zero.
